channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Matt Timoko set to return for Raiders in Auckland clash against Warriors
item.description: Matt Timoko is poised to make his NRL return for the Canberra Raiders in round two, following a pre-season foot injury that sidelined him for the season opener. The experienced Kiwi centre, who was pr...

item.title: ANC mayor reflects on Mosiuoa Lekota’s legacy at memorial service
item.description: A memorial service was held in Soweto for the late Mosiuoa Lekota, the anti-apartheid activist and former leader of the Congress of the People (COPE), who passed away last week at 77 after a long illn...

item.title: Potential Risks for Iranian Women Soccer Players and Their Families After Defection
item.description: In the wake of the Iranian women's soccer team’s defection, journalist Ben Harvey explores the tense intersection of sport and international diplomacy. Historically, high-profile defections and intern...

item.title: Sindh shifts universities to online classes amid fuel-saving measures
item.description: The Sindh government has decided that all public and private universities in the province will hold online classes from March 16 to 31 as part of a major austerity plan. This step is aimed at reducing...

item.title: Jill Biden Announces Memoir Reflecting on Her Time as First Lady and Joe's 2024 Campaign Withdrawal
item.description: Former First Lady Jill Biden has announced her new memoir, 'View from the East Wing: A Memoir,' scheduled for publication on June 2, 2026, by Gallery Books, an imprint of Simon & Schuster. In a brief ...

item.title: CMHC reports growth in Canada's housing supply driven by rentals and 'missing middle' homes in 2025
item.description: Canada's housing supply grew notably in 2025, driven primarily by record rental construction and an increase in 'missing middle' housing, according to the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation (CMHC...

item.title: Financial Institutions in Dubai and Qatar Evacuated Amid Iran-US-Israel Tensions
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Dubai and Qatar have evacuated staff following threats from Iran to target American and Israeli financial centres in its next wave of strikes. Staff at Citi and Sta...

item.title: PCSO reports no jackpot winners for March 11 lotto draws
item.description: On March 11, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that there were no jackpot winners for the major lotto draws. The Megalotto 6/45, with a jackpot prize of ₱15,000,000.00, h...

item.title: Financing Sustainable Housing Solutions for Africa’s Growing Population
item.description: Africa dey face big wahala for housing because population dey grow fast, from 1.5 billion to 2.4 billion by 2050. Right now, Africa get housing shortage of about 50 million homes. But the way we dey b...

item.title: Catherine Opie's Portraits and Interventions at the National Portrait Gallery
item.description: American photographer Catherine Opie’s latest exhibition, 'To Be Seen', at the National Portrait Gallery offers a compelling exploration of portraiture, identity, and visibility. The show begins with ...

item.title: Tanzania considers updating education law to support modern reforms
item.description: Tanzania is facing big changes in its education sector, and experts are saying the law guiding it, the Education Act of 1978, is now outdated. The country has seen big growth in schools, universities,...

item.title: Centre Reviews LPG Supply Amid West Asia Tensions, Advises States on Security and Public Awareness
item.description: The Union Home Secretary, Mr. Govind Mohan, chaired a high-level review meeting on Wednesday with all states and Union Territories regarding the LPG supply situation, especially considering recent dev...

item.title: Opposition urges govt to consult Parliament on defence policies amid regional tensions
item.description: Islamabad – On Wednesday, Tehreek Tahaffuz Ayeen-i-Pakistan (TTAP) stressed that the government must involve the opposition and Parliament when making decisions about defence agreements and policies, ...

item.title: Mbabane Highlanders expelled from Ingwenyama Cup over fan violence
item.description: Mbabane Highlanders AM, an Eswatini football club owned by South African businesswoman Shauwn Mkhize, has been expelled from the Ingwenyama Cup after being found guilty of multiple disciplinary offenc...

item.title: Roja Episode 78 Recap: Olsen Spares Homer’s Life
item.description: In Episode 78 of the popular TV series 'Roja,' the story takes an emotional turn as Olsen chooses not to end Homer’s life, showcasing a more compassionate side of his character. This pivotal moment hi...

item.title: Rising Number of Student Protests Against Immigration Enforcement in U.S. Schools
item.description: A growing wave of student protests has been recorded across the United States targeting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). According to Defending Education, an organization monitoring schools,...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Examines Financial Ties Between Carrim, Matlala and Maumela
item.description: The Madlanga commission has revealed significant financial dealings involving North West ANC regional treasurer Suliman Carrim, murder-accused Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala, and tender businessman Morgan Mau...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Laboratory Network to Boost Disease Detection and Response
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: Tanzania dey take serious steps to strengthen her national laboratory network so that disease surveillance and readiness for health threats go better. Today, health officials, governmen...

item.title: Man from New York faces charges for attempting to lure Ottawa child online
item.description: Authorities in Ottawa are investigating a case involving a man from New York who has been accused of attempting to lure a child online. The suspect allegedly made contact with the minor through digita...

item.title: Global Reactions to Mojtaba Khamenei’s Appointment as Iran’s New Supreme Leader
item.description: Following the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli air strikes, Iran’s Assembly of Experts appointed his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as the new supreme leader. The decision has drawn a str...
